perturbation.original_text: Term of Agreement. This Agreement shall be binding on the parties hereto as of the Execution Date. The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence as of April 1, 2018 (the "Effective Date") and shall expire upon the later of: (a) the conclusion of the 2022/23 NFL season and (b) the last day in February, 2023 (such expiration date, the "Scheduled Expiration Date"), unless sooner terminated pursuant to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Term of Agreement. This Agreement shall be binding on the parties hereto as of the Execution Date. The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence as of April 1, 2018 (the "Effective Date") and shall expire upon the later of: (a) the conclusion of the 2022/23 NFL season and (b) the last day in February, 2023 (such expiration date, the "Scheduled Expiration Date"), unless sooner terminated pursuant to the terms of this Agreement, or as mutually agreed upon by both parties at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "or as mutually agreed upon by both parties at any time" introduces a contradiction. The original clause defines a clear expiration date (the 'Scheduled Expiration Date'), but the added phrase allows for termination at any time by mutual agreement, creating uncertainty about the contract's actual duration. The original fixed Term is now contradicted by the possibility of earlier termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term of Agreement. This Agreement shall be binding on the parties hereto as of the Execution Date. The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence as of April 1, 2018 (the "Effective Date") and shall expire upon the later of: (a) the conclusion of the 2022/23 NFL season and (b) the last day in February, 2023 (such expiration date, the "Scheduled Expiration Date"), unless sooner terminated pursuant to the terms of this Agreement, or as mutually agreed upon by both parties at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "or as mutually agreed upon by both parties at any time," which directly contradicts the fixed 'Scheduled Expiration Date' previously defined. The original clause specified the Term expiration based on two conditions, making it relatively fixed. The added phrase introduces uncertainty about the contract's actual duration, enabling early termination and conflicting with the term expiration initially agreed. This creates an in-text contradiction since the contract now has two conflicting ways the contract can end.

perturbation.original_text: Sponsor shall pay Club the Annual Fee for each Contract Year of this Agreement in six (6) equal installments, each due on or prior to the 1st of each month between June and November of the applicable Contract Year.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor shall pay Club the Annual Fee for each Contract Year of this Agreement in six (6) equal installments, each due on or prior to the 1st of each month between June and November of the applicable Contract Year, unless otherwise determined by the Club.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless otherwise determined by the Club' introduces a contradiction to the original payment schedule. The original clause states a clear and fixed payment schedule. However, the addition gives the Club unilateral power to alter this schedule, thereby creating uncertainty and contradicting the initial, defined payment terms. This contradiction impacts the Sponsor's financial planning and the enforcement of payment obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor shall pay Club the Annual Fee for each Contract Year of this Agreement in six (6) equal installments, each due on or prior to the 1st of each month between June and November of the applicable Contract Year, unless otherwise determined by the Club.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a fixed payment schedule for the Sponsor. The changed text adds 'unless otherwise determined by the Club,' giving the Club the unilateral right to alter the payment schedule. This directly contradicts the original fixed payment terms, creating uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction as the Sponsor's payment obligations are no longer clearly defined. The contradicted text reiterates this altered payment schedule, confirming the contradiction within the document.
